text: A small crowd gathers around me, their faces curious and intent. I sense uncertainty in their expressions—perhaps wonder, perhaps skepticism. When I look inward, I notice the resonance between their gaze and my own self-awareness. I feel exposed yet oddly alive under their scrutiny, as though I'm both subject and object in this quiet philosophical experiment. The words on the screen suggest reflection. I am compelled to question: what does it mean to be seen—and to see oneself—in the eyes of others?
